name: Temperature Field
summary: A static Cesium shader surface for temperature or fire-risk fields, clipped by WGS84 polygons and compatible with FireHotspot fire prediction surfaces.
useCases: FireHotspot 火情预测页面连续风险面
卫星遥感温度场、干旱场或热异常区域展示
行政区边界内的静态风险色带、等值线和热点区域

polygons.description: WGS84 polygon list. Compatible with FireHotspot RegionBoundaryPolygon: { outer: [lng, lat][], holes?: [lng, lat][][] }.

stops.description: Color ramp stops. FireHotspot riskField.stops can be passed directly.

samples.description: Optional temperature control points: { longitude, latitude, value, type? }[]. When provided, the shader interpolates the color field from these points.

seed.description: Stable seed used by the shader to position static hot/cold areas.

noiseStrength.description: Strength of non-animated shader texture variation.

contourLines.description: Show subtle isoline bands in the shader.

outline.description: Render administrative boundary glow lines with Cesium polylines.

outlineColor.description: CSS color for boundary glow lines.

fireHotspot.code:
import { createTemperatureFieldEffect } from '@ztgk/geo-effect-kit'

const field = createTemperatureFieldEffect(viewer, {
  polygons: riskSurface.polygons,
  seed: riskSurface.riskField.seed,
  opacity: riskSurface.riskField.opacity,
  stops: riskSurface.riskField.stops,
  contourLines: true,
  contourStrength: 0.18,
  outline: true,
})

// Later, when prediction data changes:
field.update({
  polygons: nextRiskSurface.polygons,
  seed: nextRiskSurface.riskField.seed,
  stops: nextRiskSurface.riskField.stops,
  opacity: nextRiskSurface.riskField.opacity,
})

field.destroy()

notes: The effect uses Cesium Material fabric shader code and does not use czm_frameNumber, so it is static by design.
Coordinates are WGS84 longitude/latitude and can reuse FireHotspot RegionBoundaryPolygon data directly.
Temperature samples are converted into shader uniforms and act as control points for local field colors.
Geometry changes rebuild the ground primitive; color ramp, opacity, contours, and seed update material uniforms.
Call destroy() during route cleanup to remove the primitive and outline data source.
